evidence.rule: QASC independently establishes implementation assurance. DSLC consumes the current QASC result as the deployment quality gate and does not duplicate QASC scoring.

evidence.readyRule: QASC is at benchmark and every applicable control in every lane required by the release class is satisfied.
evidence.notApplicableRule: A required control may be marked not-applicable only with a rationale, named approver, and evidence of the approved applicability determination. A bare not-applicable status remains below benchmark.
evidence.releaseBoundary: Sales, legal, and communications controls block only the activation they govern. They do not block an internal engineering deployment unless the release class requires that lane or a versioned release manifest explicitly binds the control.
evidence.claimBoundary: DSLC records legal, financial, and public-communications decisions; an agent cannot make a legal determination, execute a signature, recognize revenue, approve pricing, or authorize a public claim.
evidence.segregationOfDuties: The same actor must not both originate and sovereignly approve a legal instrument, pricing exception, revenue-recognition decision, or public disclosure.

controls.evidence: DSLC runbook, rollout PRD, contract, schema, and release evaluator
